author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council
book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon XXVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If a presbyter has through ignorance contracted an illegal marriage, while he still retains the right to his place, as we have defined in the sacred canons, yet he must abstain from all sacerdotal work.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: For it is sufficient if to such an one indulgence is granted.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: For he is unfit to bless another who needs to take care of his own wounds, for blessing is the imparting of sanctification.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But how can he impart this to another who does not possess it himself through a sin of ignorance?

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Neither then in public nor in private can he bless nor distribute to others the body of Christ, [nor perform any other ministry]; but being content with his seat of honour let him lament to the Lord that his sin of ignorance may be remitted.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: For it is manifest that the nefarious marriage must be dissolved, neither can the man have any intercourse with her on account of whom he is deprived of the execution of his priesthood.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: A priest who has fallen into an illicit marriage and been deposed, may still have his seat, but only when he abstains for the future from his wickedness.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If any presbyter before his ordination had married a widow, or a harlot, or an actress, or any other woman such as are forbidden, in ignorance, he shall cease from his priesthood but shall still have his place among the presbyters.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: But such an illegitimate marriage, on account of which he was deprived of the Sacred Ministry, must be dissolved.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: The sacred canon to which the Synod here refers is number xxvij. of St. Basil in his Canonical Epistle to Amphilochius.
